summary: Base64 decode pattern in lib/mirror.js requires manual review
details: lib/mirror.js line 15 contains a Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf8') decode pattern. This primitive is commonly used legitimately (decoding configuration data, embedded assets, or response payloads) but is also used to obscure attacker-controlled strings prior to evaluation. Tracing of how the decoded bytes are consumed downstream did not complete, so the pattern cannot be definitively classified as benign or malicious from the available evidence. Manual review of lib/mirror.js is recommended to confirm whether the decoded buffer feeds into eval/Function/exec/spawn (dropper-shape) or is parsed as data (benign).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
